item.title: Elasticsearch Ingest Pipelines
item.description: Elasticsearch is an open-source search solution which is quite popular for centralzed logs ingestion. It allows logs from various different sources to be available and searchable at a centralized location. Ingesting data from various sources though, creates a problem. How do you normalize the incoming data, split it into some common fields, add or remove metadata etc? To perform all of this (and more), Logstash is the go-to solution. It can manipulate, transform incoming data before pushing it off to Elasticsearch for indexing.
